reasoning: The informal statement claims that any group G with |G| = 462 is not simple. The Lean theorem quantifies over an arbitrary type G with `[Group G]` and `[Fintype G]`, assumes `Fintype.card G = 462`, and concludes `¬ IsSimpleGroup G`. In Mathlib, `IsSimpleGroup` (from `Mathlib/GroupTheory/Subgroup/Simple.lean`) is defined as a nontrivial group whose only normal subgroups are ⊥ and ⊤, which is the standard definition of a simple group. `Fintype.card G` corresponds to |G|, and the `[Fintype G]` instance is simply the implicit finiteness already understood when writing |G| = 462. The quantifier structure ("for all groups of order 462") matches, and the conclusion matches exactly. This is a strictly faithful formalization.
